steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. Hawthorne compares a historian's knowledge to a map — accurate but "cold and naked" — and contrasts it with the warmth of a landscape painting. The main idea is about the trade-off between a historian's accuracy and engaging storytelling.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Writers of fiction possess storytelling skills that historians do not.': The passage is not about fiction writers' skills; it focuses on historians' limitations, not on comparing them to fiction writers. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The more famous a person is, the more likely that person is to attract the attention of a historian.': Fame is never mentioned in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Maps are more practical to own than paintings.': The map and painting are a metaphor, not a literal comparison of practicality. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Historians' fidelity to the truth often results in work that is less engaging than it could be.': This directly captures the passage's contrast — the historian's map is accurate but cold, lacking the charm of a painting. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
